Customer Reference Guide

zappi · Smart EV Charger

Charge smarter.
Not just faster.

zappi is an EV charger designed to work with your solar panels — automatically adjusting how it charges based on how much energy your home is generating. This guide explains how it works, what you'll see on screen, and how to set it up correctly.

Charging Modes Configuration Tool Quick Reference

Section 01

Three ways to charge

FAST Grid Priority

Charge at full speed, always

zappi behaves like any standard EV charger. It draws maximum current from the grid regardless of what your solar panels are producing. The car will charge as fast as your vehicle and supply allow — up to 7kW on single phase or 22kW on three phase. If solar is available, it will be used, but grid power fills the rest automatically.

Energy Sources

☀ Solar (if available) ⚡ Grid (fills the rest)
ECO Solar First

Solar first, grid as backup

zappi automatically adjusts the charge rate in real time based on how much surplus solar your home is generating. It prioritises solar energy, but if solar drops below the minimum needed to charge (1.4kW — the lowest the EV charging standard allows), it quietly tops up from the grid so charging never stops. Best of both worlds.

Energy Sources

☀ Solar (preferred) ⚡ Grid (fills below 1.4kW)
ECO+ Solar Only

Zero grid, no exceptions

zappi charges exclusively from surplus solar generation — energy that would otherwise be exported to the grid for minimal return. If solar drops below 1.4kW, charging pauses automatically and resumes when the sun comes back. Ideal for customers who want to maximise self-consumption and avoid grid imports entirely.

Energy Sources

☀ Solar surplus only ⏸ Pauses if insufficient solar
Why 1.4kW? The EV charging standard (IEC 61851) doesn't support charging below 1.4kW — this is a universal technical limit, not a myenergi restriction. It's the minimum current draw an EV will accept. In ECO mode, zappi supplements from the grid to maintain this floor. In ECO+ mode, it simply waits until solar exceeds it.

Section 02

What hardware does your install need?

zappi only needs CTs on the incoming mains to charge from solar.

CTs on an inverter output or battery inverter do not change how zappi charges — they only provide generation and storage data in the app. If a customer has solar but no CT on the inverter, zappi will still solar-charge correctly; they just won't see solar kW in the app.

harvi is a wireless energy sensor that transmits CT readings to zappi without extra wiring. Required when the inverter or battery is in a different physical location from zappi.

harvi

01 — Grid Supply

What is the property's grid connection type?

02 — Solar Visibility

Should solar generation appear in the app?

03 — Battery Setup

Does this install include battery storage?

Hardware Required

    What you'll see in the app

    myenergi Energy ●●●
    ECO MODE — ACTIVE

    Installation overview

    What is a CT clamp? A current transformer (CT) clamp clips around a cable without cutting into it and measures the current flowing through it. zappi uses these readings to know how much solar is being generated, how much the home is consuming, and whether battery charge or discharge is happening — so it can charge your EV at exactly the right rate.

    Section 03

    All configurations at a glance

    Full CT clamp and harvi requirements for multiphase zappi installations. All rows assume solar-aware charging is enabled.

    Grid Solar Battery Type Extra CTs harvi Notes
    Single Phase Any Any — — Built-in CT handles everything
    Three Phase None None 0 0 ECO/ECO+ works via grid CT only
    Three Phase Single Phase None 1 1 1 CT on solar feed
    Three Phase Three Phase None 3 1 1 CT per phase, 1 harvi
    Three Phase Single Phase Hybrid inverter 1 1 1 CT on inverter output covers solar + battery
    Three Phase Three Phase Hybrid inverter 3 1 3 CTs on inverter output covers solar + battery
    Three Phase Single Phase AC-coupled battery 2 2 Separate CT + harvi for battery inverter
    Three Phase Three Phase AC-coupled battery 4 2 3 solar CTs + 1 battery CT, 2 harvi

    Hybrid inverter: Solar panels and battery connect to the same inverter. One CT on the inverter's AC output measures both energy flows — no separate battery CT required.

    AC-coupled battery: The battery has its own separate inverter on its own circuit. It needs a dedicated CT clamp, and a second harvi if it's in a different location from the solar equipment.

    Third-party battery compatibility

    myenergi devices work alongside most third-party batteries via CT monitoring — they don't need to be on the same ecosystem. Your installer can confirm compatibility for your specific battery brand and model.

    Using a 3rd-party battery with myenergi devices →
